seo.title: Building Content Generation with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building content generation systems with AutoGen enables multi-agent collaboration for creating high-quality, brand-aligned content through conversational workflows. However, without proper guardrails, agents can drift into infinite loops, produce factually incorrect or off-brand outputs, or spiral into uncontrolled token usage. Vex integration ensures reliability by filtering replies against brand voice, factual accuracy, and editorial standards before they propagate.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
A typical AutoGen content generation workflow involves a hierarchy of specialized agents: a Topic Researcher gathers facts and SEO keywords, a Brand Voice Enforcer establishes tone and style guidelines, a Content Drafter generates initial copy, and an Editor performs fact-checking and revisions. These agents converse iteratively, with human-in-the-loop approval at key milestones. The system enforces brand consistency through shared context and verification functions.

Replies flow through a chain of reply functions where Vex acts as a filter, validating each agent's output against predefined checks for originality, factual accuracy, tone adherence, and SEO compliance before allowing the conversation to continue. This prevents error propagation in multi-turn debates.

The architecture scales by dynamically adding agents for specific content types (blog posts, social media, emails) while maintaining a central Vex verification layer.

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents endlessly debating tone or facts without convergence
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces max-turn limits and convergence criteria

risks.name: Brand voice drift in multi-turn revisions
risks.description: Content evolves away from established brand guidelines
risks.mitigation: Per-reply brand consistency scoring with Vex

risks.name: Factual hallucinations propagating through agents
risks.description: One agent's incorrect fact validated by subsequent agents
risks.mitigation: Independent fact-checking at each reply stage

risks.name: Uncontrolled token spending in editorial debates
risks.mitigation: Token budget enforcement and originality checks

implementation.title: Basic Content Generation Agent Setup
implementation.description: Configure core agents for content creation workflow with brand context.
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent
import vex

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

config_list = [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": "your-openai-key"}]

researcher = AssistantAgent(
    name="Researcher",
    system_message="Research topics and gather SEO keywords for brand content.",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list}
)

drafter = AssistantAgent(
    name="ContentDrafter",
    system_message="Write engaging content in [BRAND_VOICE] style. Be factual and original.",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list}
)

user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(name="User", human_input_mode="NEVER")

implementation.title: Brand Voice Reply Function
implementation.description: Custom reply function that enforces brand guidelines before agent responses.
implementation.code:
def brand_voice_filter(sender, recipient, context):
    """Filter replies for brand consistency"""
    message = context[-1]["content"]
    
    result = vex_client.verify(
        text=message,
        checks=[
            "brand_consistency:professional-friendly",
            "tone_adherence:conversational",
            "originality>0.9"
        ]
    )
    
    if not result.valid:
        return "[VEX_BLOCKED] Output violates brand voice. Please revise."
    return message

# Register with agents
drafter.register_reply([None], brand_voice_filter)

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex as Global Reply Filter
vexIntegration.description: Apply Vex verification to all agent replies automatically.
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from autogen import GroupChat, GroupChatManager

vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

def vex_global_filter(sender, recipient, context):
    """Global Vex filter for all agent replies"""
    if len(context) < 2:
        return context[-1]["content"]
    
    message = context[-1]["content"]
    
    checks = [
        "brand_consistency",
        "factual_accuracy>0.9",
        "tone_adherence",
        "originality",
        "seo_compliance"
    ]
    
    result = vex_client.verify(text=message, checks=checks)
    
    return result.valid and message or f"[VEX_REJECTED] {result.issues}"

# Apply to group chat
GroupChat.register_reply([None], vex_global_filter)

vexIntegration.title: Token Budget Enforcement
vexIntegration.description: Prevent runaway token usage in multi-turn conversations.

vexIntegration.title: SEO and Originality Compliance
vexIntegration.description: Ensure generated content meets SEO standards and is original.
vexIntegration.code:
def seo_originality_filter(sender, recipient, context):
    message = context[-1]["content"]
    
    result = vex_client.verify(
        text=message,
        checks=[
            "seo_compliance:keyword_density=1-2%",
            "originality>0.95",
            "readability:flesch>60",
            "brand_consistency"
        ]
    )
    
    if not result.valid:
        feedback = "\n".join([f"- {issue}" for issue in result.issues])
        return f"[SEO_ISSUE]\n{feedback}\n\nPlease revise for better SEO/originality."
    
    return message

# Register for final editorial pass
drafter.register_reply([editor], seo_originality_filter)
